root_text.an5.34:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ.
root_text.an5.34:1.2: Atha kho sīho senāpati yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho sīho senāpati bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an5.34:1.3: “sakkā nu kho, bhante, bhagavā sandiṭṭhikaṁ dānaphalaṁ paññāpetun”ti?
root_text.an5.34:2.2: “dāyako, sīha, dānapati bahuno janassa piyo hoti manāpo.
root_text.an5.34:2.3: Yampi, sīha, dāyako dānapati bahuno janassa piyo hoti manāpo, idampi sandiṭṭhikaṁ dānaphalaṁ.
root_text.an5.34:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, sīha, dāyakaṁ dānapatiṁ santo sappurisā bhajanti.
root_text.an5.34:3.2: Yampi, sīha, dāyakaṁ dānapatiṁ santo sappurisā bhajanti, idampi sandiṭṭhikaṁ dānaphalaṁ.
root_text.an5.34:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, sīha, dāyakassa dānapatino kalyāṇo kittisaddo abbhuggacchati.
root_text.an5.34:4.2: Yampi, sīha, dāyakassa dānapatino kalyāṇo kittisaddo abbhuggacchati, idampi sandiṭṭhikaṁ dānaphalaṁ.
root_text.an5.34:5.1: Puna caparaṁ, sīha, dāyako dānapati yaṁ yadeva parisaṁ upasaṅkamati—yadi khattiyaparisaṁ yadi brāhmaṇaparisaṁ yadi gahapatiparisaṁ yadi samaṇaparisaṁ—visārado upasaṅkamati amaṅkubhūto.
root_text.an5.34:5.2: Yampi, sīha, dāyako dānapati yaṁ yadeva parisaṁ upasaṅkamati—yadi khattiyaparisaṁ yadi brāhmaṇaparisaṁ yadi gahapatiparisaṁ yadi samaṇaparisaṁ—visārado upasaṅkamati amaṅkubhūto, idampi sandiṭṭhikaṁ dānaphalaṁ.
root_text.an5.34:6.1: Puna caparaṁ, sīha, dāyako dānapati kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an5.34:6.2: Yampi, sīha, dāyako dānapati kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjati, idaṁ samparāyikaṁ dānaphalan”ti.
root_text.an5.34:7.1: Evaṁ vutte, sīho senāpati bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an5.34:7.2: “yānimāni, bhante, bhagavatā cattāri sandiṭṭhikāni dānaphalāni akkhātāni, nāhaṁ ettha bhagavato saddhāya gacchāmi; ahaṁ petāni jānāmi.
root_text.an5.34:7.3: Ahaṁ, bhante, dāyako dānapati bahuno janassa piyo manāpo.
root_text.an5.34:7.4: Ahaṁ, bhante, dāyako dānapati; maṁ santo sappurisā bhajanti.
root_text.an5.34:7.5: Ahaṁ, bhante, dāyako dānapati; mayhaṁ kalyāṇo kittisaddo abbhuggato: ‘sīho senāpati dāyako kārako saṅghupaṭṭhāko’ti.
root_text.an5.34:7.6: Ahaṁ, bhante, dāyako dānapati yaṁ yadeva parisaṁ upasaṅkamāmi—yadi khattiyaparisaṁ yadi brāhmaṇaparisaṁ yadi gahapatiparisaṁ yadi samaṇaparisaṁ—visārado upasaṅkamāmi amaṅkubhūto.
root_text.an5.34:7.7: Yānimāni, bhante, bhagavatā cattāri sandiṭṭhikāni dānaphalāni akkhātāni, nāhaṁ ettha bhagavato saddhāya gacchāmi; ahaṁ petāni jānāmi.
root_text.an5.34:7.8: Yañca kho maṁ, bhante, bhagavā evamāha:
root_text.an5.34:7.9: ‘dāyako, sīha, dānapati kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjatī’ti, etāhaṁ na jānāmi; ettha ca panāhaṁ bhagavato saddhāya gacchāmī”ti.
root_text.an5.34:7.11: Dāyako dānapati kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjatīti.
root_text.an5.34:8.1: Dadaṁ piyo hoti bhajanti naṁ bahū,

translation_text.an5.34:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Vesālī, at the Great Wood, in the hall with the peaked roof.
translation_text.an5.34:1.2: Then General Sīha went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and asked him,
translation_text.an5.34:1.3: “Sir, can you point out a fruit of giving that’s apparent in the present life?”
translation_text.an5.34:2.2: “A giver, a donor is dear and beloved to many people.
translation_text.an5.34:2.3: This is a fruit of giving that’s apparent in the present life.
translation_text.an5.34:3.1: Furthermore, good and true persons associate with a giver.
translation_text.an5.34:3.2: This is another fruit of giving that’s apparent in the present life.
translation_text.an5.34:4.1: Furthermore, a giver gains a good reputation.
translation_text.an5.34:4.2: This is another fruit of giving that’s apparent in the present life.
translation_text.an5.34:5.1: Furthermore, a giver enters any kind of assembly bold and assured, whether it’s an assembly of aristocrats, brahmins, householders, or ascetics.
translation_text.an5.34:5.2: This is another fruit of giving that’s apparent in the present life.
translation_text.an5.34:6.1: Furthermore, when a giver’s body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.an5.34:6.2: This is a fruit of giving to do with lives to come.”
translation_text.an5.34:7.1: When he said this, General Sīha said to the Buddha,
translation_text.an5.34:7.2: “When it comes to those four fruits of giving that are apparent in the present life, I don’t have to rely on faith in the Buddha, for I know them too.
translation_text.an5.34:7.3: I’m a giver, a donor, and am dear and beloved to many people.
translation_text.an5.34:7.4: I’m a giver, and good and true persons associate with me.
translation_text.an5.34:7.5: I’m a giver, and I have this good reputation: ‘General Sīha gives, serves, and attends on the Saṅgha.’
translation_text.an5.34:7.6: I’m a giver, and I enter any kind of assembly bold and assured, whether it’s an assembly of aristocrats, brahmins, householders, or ascetics.
translation_text.an5.34:7.7: When it comes to these four fruits of giving that are apparent in the present life, I don’t have to rely on faith in the Buddha, for I know them too.
translation_text.an5.34:7.9: ‘When a giver’s body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.’ I don’t know this, so I have to rely on faith in the Buddha.”
translation_text.an5.34:7.10: “That’s so true, Sīha! That’s so true!
translation_text.an5.34:7.11: When a giver’s body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.an5.34:8.1: Giving, you’re loved and befriended by many people.
translation_text.an5.34:8.2: You get a good reputation, and your fame grows.
translation_text.an5.34:8.3: A generous man enters an assembly
translation_text.an5.34:9.1: So an astute person, seeking happiness, <j>would give gifts,
translation_text.an5.34:9.2: having driven out the stain of stinginess.
translation_text.an5.34:9.3: They live long in the Third Heaven,
translation_text.an5.34:9.4: enjoying the company of the gods.
translation_text.an5.34:10.1: Having taken the opportunity to do good, <j>when they pass from here
translation_text.an5.34:10.2: they wander radiant in the Garden of Delight.
translation_text.an5.34:10.3: There they delight, rejoice, and enjoy themselves,
translation_text.an5.34:10.4: provided with the five kinds of sensual stimulation.
translation_text.an5.34:10.5: Having practiced the word of the unattached, <j>the unaffected,
translation_text.an5.34:10.6: disciples of the Holy One rejoice in heaven.”

variant_text.an5.34:8.2: yaso ca vaḍḍhati → yasobhivaḍḍhati (bj); yasassa vaḍḍhati (sya-all, km); yasaṁ pavaḍḍhati (mr)
variant_text.an5.34:9.4: sahabyagatā ramanti te → sahabyataṁ gatā ramanti (bj); sahabyatā ramanti te (mr)
variant_text.an5.34:10.6: Ramanti sagge → kamanti sabbe (sya-all, km); ramanti sumanā (mr)

reference_text.an5.34:1.1: bj20.60, csp1ed16.33, csp2ed16.33, ms16A5_172, msdiv34, pts-vp-pli3.39
